risks.taskTitle: Create new page: Member roles developer guide

accuracy.rationale: Covers 6 distinct API surfaces (ContentAccessSettings, HasAccess(), ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames, IContentItemMemberRoleManager, IContentItemMemberRoleRetriever, ApplicationRole/ApplicationRoleStore). Writer must synthesize behavior from 3 high-significance code areas (AREA-002, AREA-006, AREA-009) and explain the three-tier access model with precise return value semantics (Allowed/NotAuthenticated/Forbidden). The lazy loading behavior of ContentItemRolesResultProcessor is a subtle implementation detail easy to get wrong.

completeness.rationale: 8 entirely new sections on a brand-new page. Must comprehensively cover the full membership role system end-to-end — from conceptual overview through API reference to ASP.NET Identity integration. This page is the canonical cross-reference target for TASK-003 through TASK-013; any gap here will cascade to downstream tasks. Breadth spans authentication, content retrieval, content queries, and programmatic management.

invariantViolation.rationale: 13 applicable invariants — the highest of any task. New page creation requires full front matter (INV-frontmatter-*), correct section structure with Introduction/Body/Result/Next Steps (INV-structure-001), persona-appropriate tone (INV-persona-*), and proper code sample formatting (INV-codesamples-*). Every structural decision is made from scratch with no existing template to follow.

dependency.rationale: No upstream dependencies. This task is a root node in the dependency graph. Can start immediately.

effortEstimation.rationale: Large complexity with 8 new sections and multiple code samples to write from scratch. No existing content to anchor effort estimates. The scope includes both conceptual explanation and detailed API reference content, making it the most ambiguous task to estimate. The writer must compose ~800-1200 words of original technical content.

mitigations: Accuracy reviewer must cross-check every API method signature against actual source files in AREA-002 (ContentAccessSettings), AREA-006 (query system), and AREA-009 (HasAccess extension)
Content writer should use the docFacts from IMP-013 as the primary source of truth for method signatures and return types — do not infer behavior from naming alone
Front matter must be validated against INV-frontmatter-001 through INV-frontmatter-008 before review cycle begins
Write the HasAccess() section first and validate its Allowed/NotAuthenticated/Forbidden return semantics against ContentItemFieldsSourceExtensions.cs before proceeding to other sections
reviewFocus: Verify three-tier model (Public/Secured/SecuredWithRoles) is described consistently with ContentAccessSettings factory method names
Check that ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames lazy loading behavior is accurately documented — only fetched when ContentItemIsSecured=true
Ensure all code samples compile conceptually — correct generic type parameters, correct extension method receiver types
Verify front matter matches INV-frontmatter requirements (identifier, order, persona fields)

accuracy.rationale: API reference page documenting a breaking change (struct-to-class migration) with 4 docFacts. Must precisely document: (1) static factory methods Public()/Secured()/SecuredWithRoles(IEnumerable<string>), (2) equality operator behavior (== and !=), (3) null validation on AccessSettings setters (ArgumentNullException), (4) value-type-to-reference-type migration implications. Any inaccuracy in the breaking change guidance could cause developers to write incorrect migration code. AREA-002 has significance=high with 3 public APIs and 1 breaking change.

completeness.rationale: 9 sections to create, but the scope is well-bounded by the API surface of a single class. Reference pages have formulaic structure. However, must cover all factory methods, operators, and the breaking change note — omitting any one creates a documentation gap. Research recommendations REC-001, REC-005, REC-012 all apply.

invariantViolation.rationale: 7 applicable invariants. New page requires full front matter and section structure. Reference pages have specific formatting requirements (INV-codesamples-*, INV-formatting-*) but are more formulaic than how-to pages. REC-005 provides additional section ordering guidance that must align with invariants.

dependency.rationale: No upstream dependencies. Root node in the dependency graph. Can start immediately.

effortEstimation.rationale: Reference pages are more formulaic than how-to guides, reducing estimation uncertainty. However, the breaking change documentation requires careful crafting of migration advice and before/after examples, which adds effort beyond a standard reference page.

mitigations: Accuracy reviewer must verify every factory method signature against the actual ContentAccessSettings.cs source in AREA-002, including parameter types and return types

The struct-to-class breaking change note must explicitly mention: default(ContentAccessSettings) no longer works, stack allocation semantics change, and null is now a valid reference — cross-check against AREA-002 breaking changes list
Use REC-012 comparison table format for the three access modes, but verify it doesn't conflict with INV-formatting invariants
Validate that equality operator documentation matches the actual == and != operator implementations in source
reviewFocus: Breaking change section: verify that every migration scenario mentioned is accurate (default value behavior, null handling, equality semantics)
Null handling: confirm that setting AccessSettings to null throws ArgumentNullException and that ContentAccessSettings.Public() is the documented replacement
Factory method signatures: exact parameter types (IEnumerable<string> for SecuredWithRoles, not string[] or List<string>)
Ensure the page follows reference page structure conventions per REC-005 and INV-structure invariants

Must replace NoOpApplicationRole→ApplicationRole and NoOpApplicationRoleStore→ApplicationRoleStore across multiple code samples with zero errors. Must add a new three-tier access model section and update the architecture section for role stores. The page is a foundational guide — errors here propagate to developer understanding. AREA-010 (Authentication and Identity Pipeline) has 1 public API and 1 breaking change.
